description.before: We solve LGSM and CFF compliance remotely and quickly. Avoid fines up to

Services.title: What you don't know can cost you.
Services.description: The LGSM, Commercial Code, and CFF establish clear obligations for every legal entity. Not knowing them does not exempt compliance, and the consequences are real.

cards.subtitle: LGSM · ARTS. 128, 194, 219
cards.title: Mandatory corporate books
cards.description: Every commercial company must keep a share registry, minutes book for shareholders' meetings, and capital variation records. It is legally mandatory.

cards.subtitle: COMMERCIAL CODE · ARTS. 33-41
cards.title: Minimum retention of 10 years
cards.description: Corporate books must be retained for at least 10 years from each entry. Mandatory even after the company has been liquidated.

cards.subtitle: CFF · ARTS. 28, 83, 84
cards.title: Fines and tax penalties
cards.description: Failing to keep corporate accounting is a tax violation punishable with fines from $1,960 to $19,530 MXN per detected infraction. No exceptions.

quote.text: Legal entities are required to keep their accounting records, including books, registries, and other corporate documentation required by commercial laws.

Process.description: Estimated fine per year of non-compliance plus updates and surcharges
Process.source: Source: Art. 83 CFF · Annex 5 RMF 2025 · Updated amounts

Pricing.description: From company formation to yearly compliance. One legal team, everything your business needs.

fundacion.title: Company formation
fundacion.description: S.A. de C.V, S. de R.L. de C.V, and other entities. Formation deed, registration, and initial books.

cumplimiento.title: Corporate books
cumplimiento.description: Draft from scratch or update: shareholder records, meeting minutes, capital changes, and board records.

gobernanza.title: Shareholders meetings
gobernanza.description: Ordinary and extraordinary meetings. Notice, minutes, and proper registration in corporate books.

urgente.title: Corporate regularization
urgente.description: Years without book updates. We rebuild your full corporate history and bring you back into compliance.

estructura.title: Corporate changes
estructura.description: Partner entry/exit, capital increase, administrator changes, and bylaw amendments.

suscripcion.title: Annual maintenance
suscripcion.description: Annual meeting, book updates, and power-of-attorney review. Keep your company compliant every year.

Steeps.description: No bureaucracy. No travel. No surprises.

quote.title: Get a quote online

proposal.title: Proposal in 24h
proposal.description: Scope, deliverables, and fixed price. No fine print, no hidden fees, no prior commitment.

delivery.title: Documents delivered
delivery.description: Everything in digital format with full legal validity. Ready for audit, bank, or notary from day one.

notes: ART. 178 LGSM - The General Shareholders' Meeting is the supreme governing body; it may approve and ratify all acts and operations of the company.
ART. 194 LGSM - Shareholders' meeting minutes must be recorded in the proper book and signed by the president, secretary, and auditors.
ART. 128 LGSM - Corporations must keep a share registry with the name, nationality, and domicile of each shareholder.
ART. 38 C.COM - Obligation to preserve books and records for a minimum period of 10 years from each registered entry.
ART. 28 CFF - Legal entities are required to keep accounting records, including books and corporate registries.
ART. 83 CFF - Failure to keep required accounting records is a tax infringement subject to fines. No exceptions.

Faq.description: Direct, practical answers to the most common questions.

items.question: What are corporate books?
items.answer: They are legal records of your company history: shareholders, meetings, capital changes, and board resolutions. They are mandatory under Mexican corporate regulations.

items.question: Am I legally required to have them?
items.answer: Yes. Every commercial entity must keep corporate books and maintain them updated to remain compliant.

items.question: What happens if they are not updated?
items.answer: You may face tax fines, administrator liability risk, delays in banking or investment processes, and more expensive future regularization.

items.question: How does the 100% remote service work?
items.answer: You share information digitally, we prepare all documents, guide you step by step, and deliver everything ready for legal use.

items.question: Do the documents have legal validity?
items.answer: Yes. Documents are prepared under current Mexican legal framework and are suitable for audits, banks, investors, or notaries.

items.question: How fast can I receive my documents?
items.answer: It depends on scope. In most cases we send a proposal within 24h and deliver documents in a few business days after confirmation.

items.question: Do you work with notaries?
items.answer: Yes. When notarization or notarial procedures are required, we coordinate the process end-to-end.
